B) Nucleotide triphosphate act as
substrate and polymerization in a
template.
C) A short stretch of RNA remains bound
to enzyme.
D) Last step is termination.
108. Initiation factor and termination factor
are- (Pg. 110, E)
A) Sigma and Rho factor respectively.
B) Rho and Sigma factor respectively.
C) Rho and Rho factor respectively.
D) Sigma and Sigma factor respectively.
109. Translation & transcription in eukaryote
occur in (Pg. 110, E)
A) Cytoplasm & nucleus respectively
B) Nucleus & cytoplasm respectively
C) Cytosol
D) Nucleus
110. Which of following can be coupled in
bacteria? (Pg. 110, E)
A) Replication & transcription
B) Transcription & translation
C) Replication & translation
D) None of these
111. Transcription of 18 s rRNA is done by
in eukaryote. (Pg. 111, E)
A) RNA pol. I B) RNA pol. II
C) RNA pol. III D) All of these
112. Choose incorrect statement. (Pg. 111, E)
A) 5.8 sr-RNA and 5 s-RNA transcribes by
same RNA polymerase in eukaryote.
B) hnRNA & mRNA transcribe by same
RNA polymerase in eukaryote.
C) tRNA & snRNA transcribes by same
RNA polymerase in eukaryote.
D) None of these
113. Splicing is required to- (Pg. 111, E)
A) remove intron in eukaryote
B) remove exon in eukaryote
C) remove exon in prokaryote
D) remove intron in prokaryote
114. Capping is- (Pg. 111, E)
A) Addition of methyl guanosine
triphosphate at 5' end
B) addition of adenylate residue at 3' end
C) addition of methyl guanosine
triphosphate at 3' end
D) addition of adenylate residue at 5' end
115. The fully processed hnRNA is- (Pg. 111, E)
A) tRNA B) mRNA
